concept: Instant / Immediate Point / Flash of Time
description: An infinitely brief period of time; a point without duration. 'Tiroe' names the mathematical point of time—the flash, the immediate, the absolutely now.
sound_symbolism: The word flashes past. 'Ti' begins with crisp precision, point defined. 'Ro' rolls through instantly. Finally 'e' opens and vanishes, the instant gone.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo tiroe' (the instant). Related to 'shemu' (moment) but emphasizes absolute brevity rather than experienced presence.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Each instant arises and passes—the flash of existence that constitutes reality.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Instants of decision shaped fate—the flash when choice was made, action taken.
